mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hike periods with statistical significance (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.2 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing investors to de-risk and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.2 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in consumer price inflation and retail sales indicate robust consumer spending power that outpaces price pressures, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55 percent of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a monthly increase in the industrial production index signals resilient manufacturing output that offsets rate hike headwinds, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening cycles fail to generate positive 5-day forward returns with a rank IC above 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate household income, driving higher consumer expenditure and corporate earnings that yield positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth correlates with negative 20-day forward returns in over 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.3 million units, construction sector contraction triggers negative wealth effects and reduced supply chain activity, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in 65 percent of quarters where housing starts remain below the 1.3 million threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates real rates are rising sufficiently to cool demand without triggering recession, creating a soft landing environment that supports positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The real rate spread signal yields negative forward returns in 70 percent of cases when inflation remains above 3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, quarterly declines in real GDP signal demand exhaustion that precedes earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDP contractions in late-cycle phases produce positive 60-day forward returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.96.
